hard.next_step: 3/16 BCE: Awaiting RFP 12/26 BCE: Awaiting RFP. Spoke with Brian, still undergoing RFP creation 10/24 BCE: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos. Making a list of requirements and a RFP. Should hear back end of October for RFP 9/29 BCE: Reaching out 9/19 BCE: Awaiting response. will reach out next EOW 9/12 BCE: Showed demo yesterday. Received well. The team is meeting internally to shortlist, should hear back soon 8/28 Meeting scheduled to restart project 8/15 BCE: Reaching out once more next week. If nothing, closing out. 7/14 Likely closeout, they have gone dark 6/30 Cory was recpetive on the intro call but has been quiet again. We need to decide if this is worth keeping in pipeline 6/9 BCE: Intro occurred. Looking at P2P, with S2P on wishlist. Currently using DDSI but exploring other options. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement. Sending follow up email to get approx. user count for BP price quote. 6/2/25 BCE: Scheduled reconnect intro with Cory and Elissa on 6/9 to align on where we are and next steps --------------------- LCS Opportunity Summary – Christian Q1–Q2 2025 Working with Cory Griffiths (Director of Procurement) and Elissa Rogers to schedule a high-level overview for executive leadership. Invite sent for 6/4 meeting. Cory confirmed ongoing internal discussions; next steps include executive alignment, high-level overview, and subsequent demo. Christian initially met with Cory on 4/2. LCS (including PCS subsidiary) expressed need for a full-suite S2P platform, preferring one provider over multiple point solutions. Areas of interest: Procurement, Sourcing, Spend Analytics, Contracts, Supplier Management, Project & Savings Management. Budget confirmed (~$150K+ SaaS, equal or more for Services); no RFP expected. Zycus references with Brookdale and Benchmark (peers in senior living) resonated. Q1 2025 Rod Copple (CFO) handed procurement ownership to Cory. Outreach began in February after his onboarding. Multiple touchpoints with Rod and Christian to schedule initial calls and align stakeholders. Procurement Pinnacle invite extended. Email and call follow-ups sent in March and April to drive meeting scheduling and share materials via Showpad. Late 2024 Opportunity stalled with Rod; strategy shifted to target executive team (CFO, CPO) using peer success stories (Brookdale, Benchmark). Rod was actively engaging with shared content but unresponsive to meeting invites. Intent observed on Showpad. RSD attempted re-engagement via direct calls, content sharing, and high-value metrics. Secure content site created for LCS review.
hard.ais_why:
• Champion Cory Griffiths engaged directly with the Autonomous Supplier Agent and Autonomous Negotiation Agent on the September 11 2025 demo, and asked specifically about Teams conversational AI integration. Initial champion Rod Copple framed the relationship around Merlin solving manual process pain. The Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA product line is in scope, with AI importance recorded as Important.
• The 84-minute LCS demo carried 13 senior attendees, with the buyer-driven closing action item being internal conversations to define specific use cases and business requirements before deeper sessions. The Avoma notes for this call are still pending generation, so direct transcript verbatim is not yet available, but the rep-captured agentic-platform notes and Gen AI commentary independently anchor the AI-led posture.
• Verbatim previously captured at action-item level; full transcript review was not possible because Avoma notes for this meeting are not yet ready. The Hungry call rests on the rep-logged champion AI engagement plus a multi-attendee senior demo with explicit AI follow-through. Confidence is moderated to Medium pending transcript-level corroboration.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / do nothing (incumbent DDSI on a self-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon)

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold: the last real buyer engagement was the 11 Sep 2025 demo (about 10 months ago); the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 never arrived, the close date has slipped four times, no economic buyer is engaged, and the June 2026 outreach drew only Brian Lambert's out-of-office auto-reply. Best Case is indefensible.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 28 Aug 2024 (about 678 days old). Formal Evaluation, close 27 Aug 2026 is about 52 days out. Last buyer-facing engagement was the 11 Sep 2025 demo, roughly 298 days ago; every touch since is rep-side (Clari emails and call attempts 15 Jun 2026) and the only buyer reply was Brian Lambert's out-of-office auto-reply. RFP promised end Oct 2025, still open at 2026-03-16, more than 8 months overdue. Close date moved 2025-10-31, 2025-12-08, 2026-02-06 and 2026-03-27 with no stage progression. RFP receipt, requirements, pricing, validation, negotiation and contracting in 52 days is not credible.
evidence: Next Step 2025-12-26: Awaiting RFP, spoke with Brian, still undergoing RFP creation
Next Step 2025-10-24: still evaluating other vendors with high-level demos, making a list of requirements and an RFP
Next Step 2025-09-12: Showed demo yesterday, received well, team meeting internally to shortlist
Task 2026-06-15: Clari email to Brian and Elisa; the only buyer reply was Brian Lambert's out-of-office auto-reply
CloseDate slips: 2025-10-31, 2025-12-08, 2026-02-06, 2026-03-27 (by Bailey Erazo / Michael McCarthy)
Avoma demo 2025-09-11: Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO) closed with 'there's probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment'

metrics.narrative: LCS articulated a qualitative case, never a quantified one. On the 11 Sep 2025 demo Elisa Baptiste (SVP Chief Operating Officer, LCS Group Purchasing) said the spend analytics piece 'is very important' and stressed that '50% of that for us comes from outside of our organization with our non-LCS related clients', framing a group-purchasing need to extend analytics value to member communities. The underlying driver is consolidation onto one full-suite Source-to-Pay provider rather than point solutions (Next Step summary, Q1-Q2 2025). No cost-of-problem, savings target, cycle-time or headcount number has ever been captured, so building a quantified savings model with LCS finance (Robbie Rushing) is a required move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been engaged on commercials, but the evidence points to one. Salesforce budget signal from Q1-Q2 2025 shows Rod Copple (Vice President, Director of Integration Services, and the SF Decision Maker) as the original budget authority who handed procurement ownership to Cory Griffiths, and the Next Step summary records budget confirmed at roughly $150K+ SaaS with equal or more for services. On the 11 Sep 2025 demo the most senior and most authoritative voice was Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO, LCS Group Purchasing), who set the agenda, gated scope ('there's probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment') and steered the analytics and integration discussion, which is the behaviour of the budget owner for the group-purchasing business unit. She has not been contacted since. Re-opening the relationship with Elisa Baptiste and confirming her as the commercial sponsor is the gating move; pricing was never taken to her.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are known at a category level but were never formalized into a scored requirement set. On the 11 Sep 2025 demo Elisa Baptiste named sourcing flow understanding, spend analytics (including outside-organization data from non-LCS members), contract management and eProcurement as the priorities, and pressed on how the platform extends to non-LCS group-purchasing clients as a 'value partnership'. Elissa Rogers (Procurement Analyst) flagged autonomous sourcing for a small team as compelling, and Robbie Rushing (Sr. Finance Manager) probed importing external member data for analytics. The buyer stated their intent to build 'a list of requirements and an RFP' (Next Step 2025-10-24), but that RFP has never been produced, so the confirmed criteria remain the demo-stated priorities; getting the written requirements is what would harden them.
sources: Avoma demo 2025-09-11 (Elisa Baptiste, Elissa Rogers, Robbie Rushing)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process stalled and has not been re-mapped. After the 11 Sep 2025 demo the team said it would 'meet internally to shortlist' and build a requirements list and RFP (Next Step 2025-09-12 and 2025-10-24), with Brian Lambert (Program Manager, IT) owning RFP creation. That RFP was still 'undergoing creation' at 2025-12-26 and 'Awaiting RFP' at 2026-03-16, and no shortlist outcome, approval chain, or timeline was ever confirmed. There is no documented decision process, approver list, or evaluation calendar on record, and no buyer has articulated one on a call. Getting the buyer to name the requirements, the shortlist status and who signs off is the move that would restore a process read.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process exists yet; the deal never reached commercials. There is no MSA, SOW, order form, legal, procurement or InfoSec activity on record, which is consistent with a Formal Evaluation deal that never advanced past a single overview demo. The Next Step summary notes 'no RFP expected' in the Q1-Q2 2025 read, yet the later buyer position (2025-10-24) was that they were building an RFP, so even the contracting path is unsettled. Visibility would come only after the buyer re-engages and names a requirements-and-procurement route; there is nothing to redline today.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is clear but not urgent. LCS runs DDSI today and is exploring alternatives (Next Step 2025-06-09), and wants a single full-suite Source-to-Pay provider instead of point solutions (Next Step summary, Q1-Q2 2025), implying fragmentation and integration pain. On the 11 Sep 2025 demo a small procurement team saw autonomous sourcing as relief for unmanaged tailspin (Elissa Rogers, Procurement Analyst: 'We're a pretty small team over in procurement, so this would definitely help us'), and Elisa Baptiste framed a group-purchasing need to extend analytics value to non-LCS member communities. The pain owners are Cory Griffiths (procurement) and Elisa Baptiste (COO). The critical weakness is urgency: the buyer set its own P2P replacement horizon at 2-3 years out (Next Step 2025-06-09), so the pain is real but not pressing.

champion.narrative: No champion has been secured. Cory Griffiths (Dir-Procurement) was the intended champion but did not attend the 11 Sep 2025 demo, showed no advocacy behaviours, and went quiet (Next Step 2025-06-30: 'Cory was receptive on the intro call but has been quiet again'; no buyer reply since). The strongest developing relationship is Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), who engaged deeply and asked the most substantive questions on the demo, but she has not been cultivated as a sponsor and has not been contacted since 11 Sep 2025. Robbie Rushing (Sr. Finance Manager) and Elissa Rogers (Procurement Analyst) engaged on the demo and are the best multi-thread footholds. What developed is executive-level interest on one call; what is missing is any internal advocacy, driving, or access being extended to the relevant stakeholder Baptiste into an executive sponsor and re-establishing live threads is prerequisite to any commercial step.

competition.narrative: The real threat is inertia, not a named rival. DDSI is the incumbent, and the buyer set its own P2P replacement horizon at 2-3 years out (Next Step 2025-06-09), so 'do nothing / status quo' is the leading alternative. Salesforce lists Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis, and the buyer confirmed in Oct 2025 they were 'still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos', but none has been buyer-preferred and there has been no competitive intel since 11 Sep 2025, so all five are dormant. Zycus's wedge is senior-living peer proof (Brookdale and Benchmark), which 'resonated', plus the Merlin agentic-sourcing and negotiation story, which landed as 'super interesting' with the small procurement team; converting that into a differentiated, quantified, phased proposal is how we displace both the incumbent and the field.

critical_signals.text: The 11 Sep 2025 overview demo landed well on Merlin and analytics, but Elisa Baptiste closed by saying there was more than LCS is ready to take on, and there has been no genuine buyer reply since.

The deciding rival is inertia: LCS runs DDSI and set its own replacement horizon at 2-3 years out, so 'do nothing' leads while Coupa, Ivalua, JAGGAER, Ariba and iCertis all sit dormant with no buyer preference.
Budget was self-described at roughly $150K SaaS plus equal services in early 2025, but pricing was never taken to the budget owner and no value case has been quantified.
SVP COO Elisa Baptiste is the most senior, most engaged voice and the likely budget owner, yet she has never been cultivated and has not been contacted since the September demo.
The buyer's own promised requirements list and RFP, owned by IT's Brian Lambert, has been outstanding since Oct 2025 and still has not been produced.

deal_movement.summary: No buyer-side movement this run. The record is unchanged since the 11 Sep 2025 demo: the promised RFP has never arrived, the close date slipped a fourth time (2026-03-27, to 27 Aug 2026), and the only 2026 activity is rep-side Clari outreach on 15 Jun 2026 that drew Brian Lambert's out-of-office auto-reply.

items.change: Rep-side re-engagement attempt: Clari emails and call attempts to Brian Lambert and Elisa Baptiste; only reply was an out-of-office auto-reply
Close date pushed 2026-06-17 to 2026-08-27 with no stage change (Bailey Erazo)
Next Step still 'Awaiting RFP'; RFP promised end Oct 2025 remains undelivered

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the strongest current threat is inertia: incumbent DDSI with the buyer's self-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon (2025-06-09) means 'do nothing' leads. Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis sit in the Salesforce field and were part of an active 'evaluating other vendors' state in Oct 2025, but none has been buyer-preferred and all are dormant after about 10 months of silence with no shortlist outcome. Zycus's edge is senior-living peer proof (Brookdale, Benchmark) and the Merlin agentic story.

competitors.name: Status quo / do nothing
competitors.quote: There's probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment.
competitors.how_we_win: Land a modular phase-1 scope (P2P or source-to-contract) with a quantified savings case so adoption feels small and urgent rather than a big-bang replacement.

competitors.name: DDSI
competitors.quote: Currently using DDSI but exploring other options. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement.
competitors.how_we_win: Displace on full-suite consolidation and senior-living peer proof (Brookdale/Benchmark); inject a compelling event to compress the 2-3 year horizon rather than let the incumbent coast.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin native agentic sourcing and no-charge supplier licensing against Coupa's cost profile once a live evaluation resumes.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with faster time-to-value and senior-living references for a small procurement team.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Position integrated intake-to-pay plus Merlin over point-suite complexity.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast ease-of-adoption and total cost for an organization of LCS's size against SAP complexity.

competitors.name: iCertis
competitors.quote: They are still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos.
competitors.how_we_win: Show CLM as one native module of a full S2P suite rather than a standalone contracting tool.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: LCS wants a single full-suite provider whose value extends to its non-LCS group-purchasing members; Zycus fits the suite and AI story, but the buyer explicitly signalled the full scope is more than they are ready to adopt now.

items.quote: Budget confirmed (~$150K+ SaaS, equal or more for Services); no pricing taken to a named budget owner.

items.quote: Pain articulated qualitatively; no savings, cycle-time or cost number captured.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, single-demo Formal Evaluation deal on a placeholder close date; the win-strategy priorities are to restart a live buyer thread, re-anchor on the most senior engaged stakeholder, and reframe scope small enough to overcome inertia.
flags: Re-open with Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), the most senior engaged voice and likely budget owner, before any further junior outreach; she has not been contacted since 11 Sep 2025.
Beat inertia, not a rival: propose a phased P2P-first landing zone with senior-living peer proof (Brookdale/Benchmark) to compress the buyer's self-stated 2-3 year horizon.
Multi-thread beyond the unresponsive Cory Griffiths and IT's Brian Lambert: cultivate Robbie Rushing (finance) for a quantified savings model and Elissa Rogers (procurement) as a coach.

items.title: SVP Chief Operating Officer

champion_strength.summary: No champion is secured. Cory Griffiths, the intended champion, skipped the Sep 2025 demo and is unresponsive. The strongest developing relationship is Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO), who engaged deeply on the demo but has not been cultivated or re-contacted since; Elissa Rogers is a positive but junior potential coach. What developed is executive-level interest on one call; what is missing is any advocacy, internal driving, or access being extended to Zycus.
champion_strength.champion: None confirmed (Elisa Baptiste is the strongest developing relationship)
champion_strength.source: Avoma demo 2025-09-11; SF Next_Step__c 2025-06-30; SF OpportunityContactRole

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin agentic-sourcing, autonomous negotiation and contract-AI narrative was delivered in full on the 11 Sep 2025 demo and drew the strongest positive reactions ('super interesting'), so AI is well positioned on content; the gap is not positioning but a dead buyer thread to carry it forward.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate is corroborated by the demo: the small procurement team saw autonomous sourcing as genuine relief for unmanaged tailspin and Elisa Baptiste probed Merlin's ability to learn from repeated classification work, so AI appetite is real but not yet converted into a buying motion.

items.detail: Cold for about 10 months since the 11 Sep 2025 demo; RFP promised end Oct 2025 never delivered and close date has slipped four times, so the 27 Aug 2026 date is a placeholder.
Likely economic buyer Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO) has never been taken commercials and has not been contacted since Sep 2025; no engaged budget owner.
Buyer signalled the full suite is more than they are ready to adopt and set a 2-3 year replacement horizon, so inertia/do-nothing is the leading outcome.
Near-term scope narrowed to P2P-first with S2P on a 2-3 year wishlist, a defensive/cost-cautious signal against the $195K full-suite value.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is dark: no genuine two-way buyer contact in about 10 months, only rep-side outreach and an out-of-office auto-reply. Verdict trajectory holds STEADY at Off Track versus the prior sweep; the single biggest blocker to a close is the absence of any live, senior buyer thread.
items: Last real buyer engagement: 11 Sep 2025 demo (about 298 days ago)

Close date slipped four times with no stage progression

Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement') narrows the near-term buy versus the original full-suite intent, and Elisa Baptiste reinforced it on the 11 Sep 2025 demo ('more than we're ready to take on at the moment'). This is a defensive, phased-adoption signal, not a competitive loss.

customer_preference.evidence: Well received but non-committal: 'there's probably a lot more here than we're ready to take on at the moment' (Elisa Baptiste, 11 Sep 2025); no Zycus-specific next step requested and no buyer contact since.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (fragmentation, small-team tailspin, extending analytics to non-LCS members); no savings, cost or cycle-time figure ever quantified.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A cold, single-demo Formal Evaluation deal: the 11 Sep 2025 overview demo landed well but the buyer has gone dark for about 10 months, the promised RFP never arrived, and the only thing between us and momentum is re-opening a live thread with the likely budget owner, SVP COO Elisa Baptiste.

win_position.text: Win sits low because this is a Formal Evaluation deal that never advanced past one overview demo, has no secured champion, and has had no genuine buyer reply since 11 Sep 2025.
The upside anchor is fit and AI appetite: LCS wants a single full-suite provider and the Merlin agentic-sourcing story landed as 'super interesting' with the small procurement team on 11 Sep 2025.
The likely economic buyer, SVP COO Elisa Baptiste, has never been taken commercials and has not been contacted since the September demo, so there is no engaged budget owner.

The leading competitor is inertia: LCS runs DDSI and set its own replacement horizon at 2-3 years out, and Elisa Baptiste said the full scope is 'more than we're ready to take on at the moment' (11 Sep 2025).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 is more than 8 months overdue and still 'Awaiting RFP' at 2026-03-16.
The close date has slipped four times (Oct 2025, Dec 2025, Feb 2026, Mar 2026) with no stage progression, so the 27 Aug 2026 date is a placeholder.
All 2026 activity is rep-side Clari outreach; the 15 Jun 2026 email to Brian Lambert and Elisa Baptiste drew only an out-of-office auto-reply.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has made no commitment beyond a well-received demo: no requested next meeting, no named dates, and the requirements/RFP they owned was never produced.
Scope narrowed defensively to P2P-first with Source-to-Pay on a 2-3 year wishlist (Next Step 2025-06-09), signalling cost-caution rather than intent to buy the full $195K suite now.

deal_risk.text: The dominant risk is no-decision: with DDSI incumbent and a self-stated 2-3 year horizon, LCS keeps managing procurement as-is and simply never signs.
Single-thread and dark: intended champion Cory Griffiths is unresponsive and no senior buyer is engaged, so there is no one inside LCS driving this forward.

customer_commitment.evidence: No buyer-requested next step, promised RFP never delivered, and buyer signalled the scope is more than they are ready to adopt (11 Sep 2025).

deal_momentum.evidence: Cold about 10 months, RFP 8+ months overdue, four close-date slips, 2026 activity all rep-side with only an out-of-office auto-reply.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing / incumbent DDSI with a 2-3 year horizon plus a single unresponsive thread and no engaged economic buyer.

items.action: Email Elisa Baptiste a phased P2P-first plan with Brookdale and Benchmark peer proof to restart the conversation.
items.trigger: Most senior engaged voice and likely budget owner, uncontacted since the demo; 'more than we're ready to take on at the moment'.
items.expected_effect: Re-opens a live senior thread and reframes scope small enough to break inertia; the only path to reviving the deal.

items.action: Offer Robbie Rushing a short savings-model session to quantify LCS and non-LCS member spend value.
items.trigger: No value case has ever been quantified; Rushing owns internal and external analytics data.
items.expected_effect: Builds the quantified business case that inertia currently defeats and adds a second live thread.

items.action: Ask Elissa Rogers for the status of LCS's requirements list and RFP and offer a template to accelerate it.
items.trigger: RFP promised end Oct 2025 by Brian Lambert never delivered; still 'Awaiting RFP' at 2026-03-16.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks the buyer-owned RFP and creates a concrete next milestone with a coach who is positive on the platform.

items.action: Set a hard 15-business-day decision on keep-or-close and, if no reply, have the owner's manager attempt one executive re-open.
items.trigger: Two prior close-out warnings ('7/14 Likely closeout, they have gone dark') and 10 months of no buyer engagement.
items.expected_effect: Forces an honest pipeline decision and moves the forecast to Pipeline if the account stays dark.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — The 2025-06-09 note ('Looking at P2P, with S2P on wishlist. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P replacement') narrows the near-term buy versus the original  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 18 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — The 2025-06-09 note ('Looking at P2P, with S2P on wishlist. Timeline would be 2-3 years out for P2P….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 1 is anchored on the stage win (18), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: LCS (Life Care Services) — Formal Evaluation · $195K · closes 27 Aug
cro_panel.intro: A cold, single-demo Formal Evaluation deal: the 11 Sep 2025 overview demo landed well but the buyer has gone dark for about 10 months, the promised RFP never arrived, and the only thing between us and momentum is re-opening a live thread with the likely budget owner, SVP COO Elisa Baptiste.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Win sits low because this is a Formal Evaluation deal that never advanced past one overview demo, has no secured champion, and has had no genuine buyer reply since 11 Sep 2025.
The upside anchor is fit and AI appetite: LCS wants a single full-suite provider and the Merlin agentic-sourcing story landed as 'super interesting' with the small procurement team on 11 Sep 2025.
The likely economic buyer, SVP COO Elisa Baptiste, has never been taken commercials and has not been contacted since the September demo, so there is no engaged budget owner.

The leading competitor is inertia: LCS runs DDSI and set its own replacement horizon at 2-3 years out, and Elisa Baptiste said the full scope is 'more than we're ready to take on at the moment' (11 Sep 2025).

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 18.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum is negative: the RFP promised for end Oct 2025 is more than 8 months overdue and still 'Awaiting RFP' at 2026-03-16.
The close date has slipped four times (Oct 2025, Dec 2025, Feb 2026, Mar 2026) with no stage progression, so the 27 Aug 2026 date is a placeholder.
All 2026 activity is rep-side Clari outreach; the 15 Jun 2026 email to Brian Lambert and Elisa Baptiste drew only an out-of-office auto-reply.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The dominant risk is no-decision: with DDSI incumbent and a self-stated 2-3 year horizon, LCS keeps managing procurement as-is and simply never signs.
Single-thread and dark: intended champion Cory Griffiths is unresponsive and no senior buyer is engaged, so there is no one inside LCS driving this forward.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Recency-weighted, the strongest current threat is inertia: incumbent DDSI with the buyer's self-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon (2025-06-09) means 'do nothing' leads.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: The buyer has made no commitment beyond a well-received demo: no requested next meeting, no named dates, and the requirements/RFP they owned was never produced.
Scope narrowed defensively to P2P-first with Source-to-Pay on a 2-3 year wishlist (Next Step 2025-06-09), signalling cost-caution rather than intent to buy the full $195K suite now.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Email Elisa Baptiste a phased P2P-first plan with Brookdale and Benchmark peer proof to restart the conversation. (by 13 Jul)
Offer Robbie Rushing a short savings-model session to quantify LCS and non-LCS member spend value. (by 20 Jul)
Ask Elissa Rogers for the status of LCS's requirements list and RFP and offer a template to accelerate it. (by 24 Jul)

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript or notes available (not recorded / empty)
No transcript or notes available (not recorded / empty)

gaps: No genuine two-way buyer engagement since the 11 Sep 2025 demo; 2026 touches are all rep-side and the only reply was an out-of-office auto-reply.
Buyer-owned requirements list and RFP never produced, so decision criteria and process remain unformalized.
No quantified value case captured; economic buyer never taken commercials.
The two 2025-04-02 intro calls have no transcript/notes, so early qualification detail is limited to the Next Step summary.
